problem: What substrate allows silicon-level performance in fully flexible electronics?
mechanism: Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure)
hypothesis: The key to What substrate allows silicon-level performance in fully flexible electronics? is Two-dimensional material stacking (van der Waals heterostructure) — specifically Two-dimensional material stacking — which would help 500,000,000 people affected.

experiment.protocol: Synthesis and characterization with 16 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Nature uses only the longest threads to weave her patterns. — Richard Feynman
